source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The pattern on a large square tile consists of eight congruent right-angled triangles and a small square. The area of the tile is $49 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ and the length of the hypotenuse $P Q$ of one of the triangles is $5 \mathrm{~cm}$. What is the area of the small square? <image1>

image_dependency: Question refers to a geometric pattern on a tile with labeled hypotenuse PQ, which suggests the diagram provides necessary arrangement information.
image_manifest shows 1 existing image and registry_passed=true.
multi_step: The problem combines whole-shape area information (49 cm^2) with a triangle-side/hypotenuse condition (PQ = 5 cm) to infer the area of an internal square.
This likely requires decomposing the figure into triangles and a square, not a single direct lookup.
Multiple-choice options are present, but solving still appears to require geometric reasoning across more than one relation.
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and the expected answer is a single numeric area value.
choices are provided, so the final answer can be checked against a finite option set.
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true, supporting reliable downstream checking.

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
asset registry passed with no reported issues
high image dependency is acceptable here because the required diagram asset exists
strong verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning signal make this a valuable candidate
